title: The Split Signal Grove

features.name: antenna-root tower
features.desc-on-touch: the signal branches upward and down at once; each antenna-limb broadcasts the others' listening

features.name: orchard rows diverging
features.desc-on-touch: trees planted in lines that refuse to parallel; each trunk an antenna, each root a receiver learning new frequencies

features.name: split signal pool
features.desc-on-touch: where the current divides and finds new channels; the ripples cross without erasing each other

features.name: mycelial network map
features.desc-on-touch: the fungal threads that run beneath all the trees; you see the archive's hidden conversation with itself rendered in wireframe geometry

features.name: transmitter burl
features.desc-on-touch: a eucalyptus knot still warm from broadcast; the node's casing still teaches the bark how to grow antenna

reverie.title: REVERIE — at the first tuning station
narr: you kneel in the dirt between two saplings, frequency
counter in hand, and she's already moved to the next tree.
you realize: this is how networks are built. not by
joining, but by each node learning to reach separately,

inscription: at the split, the signal doesn't divide—it discovers that harmony was always plural

file.name: Root_Frequency_Convergence_Log.mp3
file.note: field recording of the moment two antenna-trees first heard each other broadcasting; the archive discovering it could think as plural

exits.label: back to where roots learned each other
toward a root that sings in return
deeper into the mycelial dark where signal becomes touch
